file_purpose: Formalizes the IND-CCA2 security of the ChaChaPoly AEAD scheme (ChaCha20 CTR-mode encryption + Poly1305 MAC) for bounded nonce-respecting adversaries. The main theorem reduces the CCA advantage to the PRP/PRF distinguishing advantage of chacha20_block, the CPA advantage against random encryption, and two information-theoretic forgery bounds involving the Poly1305 collision probability and tag-guessing probability.

main_theorem.name: conclusion
main_theorem.informal_statement: For any bounded nonce-respecting adversary A (at most qenc encryption queries, qdec decryption queries), the CCA winning probability against ChaChaPoly is at most: the CPA advantage of A against EncRnd (uniformly random encryption), plus the PRP/PRF distinguishing advantage of chacha20_block, plus qdec times max(pr_zeropol, pr1_poly_out), plus qdec times pr1_poly_out.

proof_strategy_overview: The proof decomposes the CCA advantage via a four-step game-hopping sequence. Step 1 replaces the concrete chacha20_block with a lazy random oracle via a PRP/PRF switch, expressing the gap as the indistinguishability advantage of D(A) against IndBlock vs IndRO. Step 2 decomposes the resulting RO-based CCA game into a CPA component and a UFCMA forgery component using the generic CCA_CPA_UFCMA framework, then rewrites both in terms of split random oracles that separate the poly_in, poly_out, and extra_block randomness (games G8 and G9). Step 3 shows that under the fully split RO and nonce-respecting constraints, the CPA game (G8) is identical to encryption with uniformly random output (EncRnd), since each fresh nonce's RO entries are independent uniform samples. Step 4 bounds the UFCMA game (G9) by analyzing two bad events: bad1 (an encryption tag collides with a previously seen decryption tag, bounded by qdec * pr1_poly_out via a union bound) and bad2 (a forgery succeeds during verification, bounded by qdec * max(pr_zeropol, pr1_poly_out) via the Poly1305 collision bound).

game_chain.description: CCA_game(A, RealOrcls(ChaChaPoly)): standard IND-CCA2 game where encryption and decryption use concrete chacha20_block keyed by a uniformly random key.
Indist.Distinguish(D(A), IndBlock): indistinguishability game where D(A) wraps the CCA adversary and IndBlock provides keyed chacha20_block; the real side of the PRP/PRF switch.
Indist.Distinguish(D(A), IndRO): indistinguishability game where IndRO provides a lazy random oracle on (nonce, counter) pairs; the ideal side of the PRP/PRF switch.
CCA_game(A, RealOrcls(OChaChaPoly(IFinRO))): CCA game where chacha20_block is replaced by a finite eager random oracle, giving operator-based encryption and MAC.
CPA_game(CCA_CPA_Adv(A), RealOrcls(GenChaChaPoly(CCRO(FinRO)))): encryption-only component after CCA-to-CPA+UFCMA decomposition, using the RO-backed GenChaChaPoly.
UFCMA_poly(A, FinRO): forgery-checking component after CCA decomposition; runs CPA game then tests whether any decryption ciphertext's Poly1305 tag is valid.

G8(A, RO1) wrapped in Split1/SplitC2: CPA game with RO fully split — domain split separates counter=0 (MAC key) from counter>=1 (stream), codomain split separates block into (poly_in, poly_out, extra_block).
G9(A, RO1) wrapped in Split1/SplitC2: UFCMA forgery check with the same RO splitting as G8; iterates over nonces and checks tag validity under the split Poly1305 key material.

CPA_game(CCA_CPA_Adv(BNR_Adv(A)), EncRnd): CPA game where encryption returns uniformly random ciphertext bytes and a uniformly random Poly1305 tag, independent of the plaintext; decryption always returns None.

lemma_catalog.name: map2_zip, size_map2, nth_map2
lemma_catalog.narrative: Algebraic properties of the map2 operator: equivalence with map-over-zip, size equals min of input sizes, and element-wise access at valid indices.

lemma_catalog.name: Byte.xorK1
lemma_catalog.narrative: Byte-level XOR self-inverse: b1 = b1 +^ b2 +^ b2. Foundation for block-level XOR cancellation used throughout.

lemma_catalog.name: GenBlock.dblock_ll, GenBlock.dblock_uni, GenBlock.dblock_fu, GenBlock.dblock_funi
lemma_catalog.narrative: Distribution properties of dblock: lossless, uniform, full, and funiform. Inherited by all cloned block types (dpoly_in, dpoly_out, dpoly, dextra_block, dblock) and used pervasively in sampling arguments.

lemma_catalog.name: GenBlock.nth_xor, GenBlock.nth_zero, GenBlock.Axioms.addmA, GenBlock.Axioms.addmC, GenBlock.Axioms.add0m, GenBlock.addK, GenBlock.xorK1
lemma_catalog.narrative: Block-level XOR algebra: element-wise XOR, zero element, associativity, commutativity, identity, self-cancellation, and self-inverse. Establishes blocks as an abelian group under XOR, critical for proving CTR encryption/decryption invertibility in dec_enc.

lemma_catalog.name: C.inhabited, C.enum_spec, GenBlock.inhabited
lemma_catalog.narrative: Clone realization obligations: counter subtype is inhabited, counter enumeration is correct, and block subtype is inhabited (via nseq of zero bytes).

lemma_catalog.name: nth_extend
lemma_catalog.narrative: Element-wise characterization of the extend operation: for i < size m returns the original byte, otherwise returns Byte.zero. Used in XOR equivalence proofs.

lemma_catalog.name: take_xor_map2_xor
lemma_catalog.narrative: Proves map2_xor and take_xor compute the same result on any message and stream block. Justifies using either XOR formulation interchangeably in CTR-mode proofs.

lemma_catalog.name: iter_gen_ctr_round_nil_cat, iter_gen_ctr_round_S, iter_gen_ctr_round_nil
lemma_catalog.narrative: Structural lemmas for iterating gen_ctr_round: prefix concatenation distributes over iteration, step-unfolding exposes one encryption round, and iteration on empty input is a no-op.

lemma_catalog.name: gen_CTR_encrypt_bytes0, gen_CTR_encrypt_bytes_cons
lemma_catalog.narrative: Base and recursive cases of gen_CTR_encrypt_bytes: empty plaintext yields empty ciphertext; non-empty plaintext XORs one block and recurses on the remainder with incremented counter.

lemma_catalog.name: OpCC.chacha_spec
lemma_catalog.narrative: Phoare spec for ChaCha(OCC(I)).enc: terminates with probability 1 and returns gen_CTR_encrypt_bytes applied to the operator cc. Used to bridge the procedural while-loop implementation to its functional specification.
lemma_catalog.semantic_delta: Proves the procedural while-loop in ChaCha(OCC(I)).enc computes exactly gen_CTR_encrypt_bytes(take_xor, cc gs, k, n, 1, p); purely a procedure-to-operator correspondence, no game-level change.

lemma_catalog.name: OpCC.poly_spec
lemma_catalog.narrative: Phoare spec for Poly(OCC(I)).mac: terminates with probability 1 and returns genpoly1305(cc gs, k, n, topol a c). Bridges the procedural MAC computation to its functional specification.
lemma_catalog.semantic_delta: Proves Poly(OCC(I)).mac computes exactly genpoly1305(cc gs, k, n, topol a c); procedure-to-operator correspondence.

lemma_catalog.name: OpCC.CCP_OCCP
lemma_catalog.narrative: Equivalence between A(GenChaChaPoly(OCC(I))).main and A(OChaChaPoly(I)).main: the procedural wrapper and the operator-based wrapper give identical results for any adversary A. The core building block for pr_CCP_OCCP.
lemma_catalog.semantic_delta: Replaces procedural oracle calls in GenChaChaPoly(OCC(I)) — ChaCha while-loop and Poly oracle call — with direct operator evaluation in OChaChaPoly(I), preserving all inputs and outputs.

lemma_catalog.name: OpCC.pr_CCP_OCCP
lemma_catalog.narrative: Probability-level consequence of CCP_OCCP: Pr[A(GenChaChaPoly(OCC(I))).main()] = Pr[A(OChaChaPoly(I)).main()]. Used in step1 to switch between the procedural and functional formulations of ChaChaPoly. Instantiated via OpCCinit (with chacha20_block) and OpCCRO (with RO map lookup).
lemma_catalog.semantic_delta: Replaces the procedural GenChaChaPoly(OCC(I)) wrapper with the functional OChaChaPoly(I) wrapper; no change in game structure, adversary interface, or underlying block function — purely syntactic refactoring of how encryption and MAC are computed.

closer_hints.typical_tail: byequiv => //; proc; inline *; wp; call (_: Mem.k{1} = IndBlock.k{2}); last by auto.

lemma_catalog.name: Step1_2.dec_enc
lemma_catalog.narrative: Realizes the dec_enc axiom for CCA_CPA_UFCMA: decryption correctly inverts encryption under take_xor CTR mode. Proved by strong induction on plaintext size using XOR self-cancellation (Byte.xorK1).

lemma_catalog.name: SplitC1.topairK, SplitC1.sample_spec, SplitC2.topairK, SplitC2.sample_spec
lemma_catalog.narrative: Clone realizations for the two codomain-splitting layers. SplitC1 splits block into (poly, extra_block); SplitC2 splits poly into (poly_in, poly_out). Each proves the topair/ofpair bijection and that sampling the joint distribution equals sampling the product of marginals.

lemma_catalog.name: poly_mac1
lemma_catalog.narrative: Local equiv: Poly(OpCCinit.OCC(I_stateless)).mac ~ D(A, IndBlock).O.Poly.mac when k{1} = IndBlock.k{2}. Used in step1 to relate the real ChaChaPoly scheme to D's internal Poly oracle on the IndBlock side.
lemma_catalog.semantic_delta: Shows the Poly1305 MAC under OCC(I_stateless) with key k produces the same tag as D(A, IndBlock).O.Poly.mac when IndBlock.k = k; both compute poly1305(r, s, topol a c) using chacha20_block(k, n, 0) to derive (r, s).
lemma_catalog.invariant_sketch: Pre: k{1} = IndBlock.k{2} /\ ={n, a, c}. Post: ={res}. Use inside a pRHL goal where the enclosing `call (_: Mem.k{1} = IndBlock.k{2})` invariant already establishes the key equality.

lemma_catalog.name: chacha_enc1
lemma_catalog.narrative: Local equiv: ChaCha(OpCCinit.OCC(I_stateless)).enc ~ D(A, IndBlock).O.ChaCha.enc when k{1} = IndBlock.k{2}. Used alongside poly_mac1 in step1 for the real-block-cipher side.
lemma_catalog.semantic_delta: Shows ChaCha CTR encryption under OCC(I_stateless) with key k produces the same ciphertext as D(A, IndBlock).O.ChaCha.enc when IndBlock.k = k; both XOR plaintext with chacha20_block(k, n, counter) blocks.
lemma_catalog.invariant_sketch: Pre: k{1} = IndBlock.k{2} /\ ={n, p}. Post: ={res}. Use inside a pRHL goal where the enclosing `call (_: Mem.k{1} = IndBlock.k{2})` invariant already establishes the key equality.

lemma_catalog.name: poly_mac2
lemma_catalog.narrative: Local equiv: Poly(OCC(IFinRO)).mac ~ D(A, G2(FinRO).CCRO).O.Poly.mac when OCC.gs{1} = RO.m{2}. Used in step1 for the random-oracle side.
lemma_catalog.semantic_delta: Shows the Poly1305 MAC under OCC backed by a finite RO state produces the same tag as D's internal Poly oracle backed by the same RO map; both look up (n, 0) in the shared map to derive (r, s).
lemma_catalog.invariant_sketch: Pre: OCC.gs{1} = RO.m{2} /\ ={n, a, c}. Post: ={res}. Use inside the `call (_: OCC.gs{1} = RO.m{2})` enclosing invariant on the RO-backed side of step1.

lemma_catalog.name: chacha_enc2
lemma_catalog.narrative: Local equiv: ChaCha(OCC(IFinRO)).enc ~ D(A, G2(FinRO).CCRO).O.ChaCha.enc when OCC.gs{1} = RO.m{2}. Used alongside poly_mac2 in step1 for the random-oracle side.
lemma_catalog.semantic_delta: Shows ChaCha CTR encryption under OCC backed by a finite RO state produces the same ciphertext as D's internal ChaCha oracle backed by the same RO map.
lemma_catalog.invariant_sketch: Pre: OCC.gs{1} = RO.m{2} /\ ={n, p}. Post: ={res}. Closing the call residual uses `while (={p, c, n, i} /\ OCC.gs{1} = RO.m{2}); auto.` for the CTR loop.

closer_hints.typical_tail: while (={p, c, n, i} /\ OCC.gs{1} = RO.m{2}); auto.

lemma_catalog.name: step1
lemma_catalog.narrative: The PRP/PRF switch: Pr[G_real] - Pr[G_ro] = Pr[G_indist_real] - Pr[G_indist_ro]. Shows the gap between the real CCA game and the RO-based CCA game equals exactly the indistinguishability advantage of D(A) against chacha20_block. Uses poly_mac1/chacha_enc1 for the real side and poly_mac2/chacha_enc2 plus pr_CCP_OCCP for the RO side.
lemma_catalog.semantic_delta: Replaces the keyed chacha20_block in ChaChaPoly with a lazy random oracle on (nonce, counter) pairs; the probability gap is captured exactly by the PRP/PRF distinguishing game.

lemma_catalog.name: step2_1
lemma_catalog.narrative: CCA-to-CPA+UFCMA decomposition: Pr[G_ro] <= Pr[CPA(StLSke(St))] + Pr[UFCMA(A, St) with successful dec]. Applies the generic CCA_CPA_UFCMA framework to the RO-based ChaChaPoly, splitting the CCA advantage into an encryption-only component and a forgery component.

lemma_catalog.name: UFCMA_genCC
lemma_catalog.narrative: Equiv between UFCMA(A, St).main and CPA_game(CCA_CPA_Adv(A), RealOrcls(GenChaChaPoly(CCRO(FinRO)))).main, preserving Mem.lc and relating StLSke.gs to RO.m. Bridges the state-based formulation from step2_1 to the RO-based formulation needed in step2_2.
lemma_catalog.semantic_delta: Shows UFCMA(A, St) — which initializes FinRO and stores RO.m as StLSke state — runs identically to CPA with GenChaChaPoly(CCRO(FinRO)), since both use the same underlying random oracle for encryption and MAC.

lemma_catalog.name: step2_2
lemma_catalog.narrative: Refines the CPA + UFCMA bound from step2_1: Pr[CPA(StLSke(St))] + Pr[UFCMA existential dec] <= Pr[CPA(GenChaChaPoly(CCRO(FinRO)))] + Pr[UFCMA_poly(A, FinRO)]. Replaces the abstract UFCMA decryption-success event with UFCMA_poly's concrete Poly1305 forgery check, using UFCMA_genCC to align the two formulations.

lemma_catalog.name: step2_3
lemma_catalog.narrative: Rewrites Pr[G_cpa] + Pr[G_ufcma_poly] as Pr[G8] + Pr[G9] by applying the domain split (SplitD separates counter=0 from counter>=1) and two codomain splits (SplitC1 separates poly from extra_block; SplitC2 separates poly_in from poly_out). This separation is essential for step3 and step4 which analyze G8 and G9 independently.

lemma_catalog.name: step2
lemma_catalog.narrative: Combines step1, step2_1, step2_2, and step2_3 into the main reduction: Pr[G_real] <= Pr[G8] + Pr[G9] + (Pr[G_indist_real] - Pr[G_indist_ro]). This is the public lemma exported from the Step1_2 abstract theory, reducing CCA security to the split-RO games plus PRP/PRF advantage.

lemma_catalog.name: pr_TPI_ok, filter_test_poly_in, pr_TPI_ok_filter
lemma_catalog.narrative: Probability bounds on test_poly_in: the probability that a random poly_in key r creates a forgery match against any ciphertext in lc is at most k * pr_zeropol (where k bounds the list size). The filter variant restricts to ciphertexts matching a specific nonce. Used in step4_bad2.

lemma_catalog.name: mk_rs_ofpair
lemma_catalog.narrative: Shows mk_rs(SplitC1.ofpair(SplitC2.ofpair(r, s), e)) = (r, s): extracting the Poly1305 key from a reconstructed block recovers the original (poly_in, poly_out) components. Used in step3 to relate the split-RO representation to mk_rs.

lemma_catalog.name: equ_cc
lemma_catalog.narrative: Local equiv showing ChaCha encryption under the fully split RO produces the same output as EncRnd.cc when the nonce is fresh (not in BNR.lenc). Core building block for step3: for fresh nonces, the split-RO at counter>=1 returns independent uniform blocks, so XOR encryption yields uniform ciphertext.
lemma_catalog.semantic_delta: Under a fresh nonce not in BNR.lenc, the split-RO ChaCha encryption samples fresh uniform blocks from ROF for each counter>=1, making the XOR ciphertext equivalent to EncRnd.cc's direct uniform byte sampling.
lemma_catalog.invariant_sketch: ecall yields an ambient residual — the current pRHL state must satisfy equ_cc's precondition (nonce fresh: not in BNR.lenc on side 1). Discharge by `rewrite /check_plaintext; auto => /> /#` (unfolds the freshness predicate so /# closes).

lemma_catalog.name: step3
lemma_catalog.narrative: Bridges G8 and G_encrnd: Pr[G8(BNR_Adv(A))] = Pr[CPA(EncRnd)]. Under the fully split RO with nonce-respecting constraints, each encryption query uses a fresh nonce, so the stream blocks and poly_out key are independent uniform samples, making the CPA game identical to random encryption.
lemma_catalog.semantic_delta: Replaces the split-RO-based CPA encryption — which XORs plaintext with fresh RO blocks at counter>=1 and computes Poly1305 with fresh poly_in/poly_out from counter=0 — with EncRnd's direct uniform ciphertext and tag sampling, exploiting nonce-freshness and independence of the split RO components.

lemma_catalog.name: step4_1
lemma_catalog.narrative: Decomposes the UFCMA forgery probability: Pr[G9(BNR_Adv(A))] <= Pr[UFCMA(ROIN.RO) : res or bad2] + Pr[UFCMA(ROIN.RO) : bad1]. Introduces the local UFCMA module with bad events: bad1 tracks whether an encryption tag collides with a decryption tag, bad2 tracks whether the forgery check succeeds for nonces whose poly_out was not yet queried.

lemma_catalog.name: equiv_step4
lemma_catalog.narrative: Restructuring equiv between UFCMA and UFCMA3: separates the single nonce-iteration loop into two phases and makes the disjunction explicit. Used to enable independent analysis of bad1 and bad2.
lemma_catalog.semantic_delta: Splits the single forgery-check loop in UFCMA.distinguish into two sequential loops in UFCMA3 — first over nonces with poly_out cached in ROout.m, then over nonces without — and tracks UF.forged and UFCMA.bad2 as separate flags.

lemma_catalog.name: step4_bad2
lemma_catalog.narrative: Bounds Pr[UFCMA(ROIN.RO) : res or bad2] <= qdec * max(pr_zeropol, pr1_poly_out). For each of at most qdec decryption ciphertexts, the forgery probability is bounded by the maximum of the Poly1305 collision probability (pr_zeropol, from pr_TPI_ok) and the tag-guessing probability (pr1_poly_out, from bad2's uniform sampling).

lemma_catalog.name: make_lbad1_size_cons2, leq_make_lbad1, make_lbad1_size_cons3
lemma_catalog.narrative: Size-tracking lemmas for make_lbad1: adding a ciphertext increments the list by 1 if its nonce matches; overall size is bounded by |lc|; adding a new nonce to lenc contributes exactly the count of matching ciphertexts. Used in the bad1 invariant proofs.

lemma_catalog.name: step4_bad1_lbad1
lemma_catalog.narrative: Reduces Pr[UFCMA bad1] to Pr[collision in UFCMA_l.lbad1]: if bad1 fires, there exists a pair (t, t') in the list lbad1 of (encryption-tag, decryption-tag) pairs with t = t'. Introduces the UFCMA_l module that records these pairs explicitly.

lemma_catalog.name: step4_lbad1_sum
lemma_catalog.narrative: Union bound over lbad1: Pr[exists collision in lbad1 of size <= qdec] <= sum_{i=0}^{qdec-1} Pr[i-th entry collides]. Uses the EventPartitioning/ListPartitioning framework to decompose the existential event.

lemma_catalog.name: step4_badi
lemma_catalog.narrative: For each index nth0 in [0, qdec), bounds Pr[nth0-th lbad1 entry collides] <= Pr[UFCMA_li.f(nth0) : badi]. Introduces UFCMA_li which isolates a single collision check into its own bad event flag.

lemma_catalog.name: pr_step4_badi
lemma_catalog.narrative: Bounds Pr[UFCMA_li.f(nth0) : badi] <= pr1_poly_out for each index: the encryption tag t is a fresh uniform sample from dpoly_out, independent of the fixed decryption tag, so Pr[t = target] = mu1(dpoly_out, witness) = pr1_poly_out.

lemma_catalog.name: step4_bad1
lemma_catalog.narrative: Combines step4_bad1_lbad1, step4_lbad1_sum, step4_badi, and pr_step4_badi: Pr[UFCMA(ROIN.RO) : bad1] <= qdec * pr1_poly_out. The qdec factor comes from the union bound over at most qdec (encryption-tag, decryption-tag) pairs.

lemma_catalog.name: conclusion
lemma_catalog.narrative: The final security bound combining all four steps: Pr[CCA(BNR_Adv(A), ChaChaPoly)] <= Pr[CPA(A, EncRnd)] + PRP/PRF advantage + qdec * max(pr_zeropol, pr1_poly_out) + qdec * pr1_poly_out. Assembles Step1_2.step2 (instantiated with BNR_Adv(A)), step3, step4_1, step4_bad2, and step4_bad1 via triangle-inequality arithmetic.

glossary.CCA_game(A, O): Standard IND-CCA2 game: adversary A receives encryption and decryption oracles from O and outputs a boolean guess. Defined in the imported Ske library.
glossary.CPA_game(A, O): IND-CPA game: adversary A receives only the encryption oracle from O (no decryption). Used after the CCA-to-CPA+UFCMA decomposition.
glossary.CCA_CPA_Adv(A): Generic reduction converting a CCA adversary A into a CPA adversary by removing decryption access; defined in the Ske/CCA_CPA_UFCMA framework.
glossary.BNR(O) / BNR_Adv(A): Bounded Nonce-Respecting wrapper: enforces at most qenc encryption queries with distinct nonces, at most qdec decryption queries, and valid_topol size constraints. BNR_Adv wraps an adversary A with these checks.
glossary.ChaChaPoly: Concrete AEAD module: encrypts via gen_CTR_encrypt_bytes(take_xor, chacha20_block, k, n, 1, p) and authenticates via genpoly1305(chacha20_block, k, n, topol(a, c)).
glossary.GenChaChaPoly(CC): ChaChaPoly parameterized by an abstract FCC module CC; allows swapping chacha20_block for a random oracle or other block function.
glossary.OChaChaPoly(I): Operator-based ChaChaPoly that computes enc/dec via direct functional evaluation of gen_CTR_encrypt_bytes and genpoly1305 on operator cc, avoiding oracle calls.
glossary.OpCC / OCC(I): Abstract theory providing an operator-based CC module; OCC.cc returns cc(gs, k, n, c) where gs is global state initialized by I. Cloned as OpCCinit (cc = chacha20_block) and OpCCRO (cc = oget map[(n,c)]).
glossary.D(A, RO): Distinguisher wrapping CCA adversary A and oracle RO into the Indistinguishability game format; internally provides CCA enc/dec using RO.f as the block function (key is ignored).
glossary.IndBlock / IndRO: Real and ideal oracles for the PRP/PRF indistinguishability game: IndBlock returns chacha20_block(k, n, c) for random k; IndRO returns lazy RO samples.
glossary.CCRO(RO): Adapter wrapping a random oracle RO into the FCC interface; cc(k, n, c) ignores the key and returns RO.get(n, c).
glossary.EncRnd: Ideal encryption module: enc samples random bytes (same length as plaintext) via dblock and a random tag via dpoly_out; dec always returns None.
glossary.StLSke(St) / St: Stateful SKE wrapper for the CCA_CPA_UFCMA framework: St.init runs FinRO.init and returns RO.m as global state; StLSke evaluates enc/dec as operators over that stored map.
glossary.UFCMA_poly(A, RO): Concrete UFCMA game: runs the CPA game, then for each nonce in the decryption log, queries RO at counter 0, extracts (r,s), and checks test_poly for tag validity.
glossary.test_poly_in: Predicate testing whether a random poly_in key r causes any decryption ciphertext's tag to be a valid Poly1305 forgery relative to an encryption ciphertext; used in the UFCMA analysis.
glossary.SplitD / SplitC1 / SplitC2: Three RO-splitting layers: SplitD separates domain by counter (0 vs non-0); SplitC1 splits counter-0 codomain into (poly, extra_block); SplitC2 splits poly into (poly_in, poly_out). Enables independent analysis of MAC key components.
glossary.G4 through G9: Intermediate game modules: G4/G5 are CPA/UFCMA over a single RO; G6/G7 add domain splitting; G8/G9 add codomain splitting. G8 is the CPA component and G9 is the UFCMA component in the final reduction.
glossary.pr_zeropol: Upper bound on Pr[poly1305_eval(r, p2) - poly1305_eval(r, p1) = target] for random r and distinct polynomials p1 != p2; the information-theoretic Poly1305 collision probability.
glossary.pr1_poly_out: mu1(dpoly_out, witness) = 1/|poly_out|; probability of guessing a uniformly random poly_out value.
glossary.mk_rs(b): Extracts the Poly1305 key pair (r : poly_in, s : poly_out) from block b by taking the first poly_in_size bytes as r and the next poly_out_size bytes as s.

synthetic_module_bridges.name: ChaChaPoly_to_OChaChaPoly_I_stateless
synthetic_module_bridges.relation: ChaChaPoly ≡ OChaChaPoly(I_stateless) — both use chacha20_block directly
synthetic_module_bridges.closer_hint: byequiv=>//; proc; inline *; wp; call (_: ={Mem.k}); sim />

synthetic_module_bridges.name: OChaChaPoly_IFinRO_to_OCCRO_IFinRO
synthetic_module_bridges.relation: Same functional form, different theory import path
